flashcards.definition: A state where forward and reverse reactions occur at equal rates; concentrations stay constant.
If a system at equilibrium is stressed, it shifts to partially counteract the stress.
A reaction that can proceed in both forward and reverse directions: A + B ⇌ C + D.
The ratio of product concentrations to reactant concentrations at equilibrium.
Adding reactant → shifts right. Adding product → shifts left. Temperature/pressure also cause shifts.
